spec_match.rationale: Renders the `<controlledframe>` declaration with `partition`, `src`, `loadstop`, `permissionrequest`, `newwindow` event hooks — exactly the spec surface.

interactive.rationale: Four configurable fields (URL/partition/nav policy/autoreload), three action buttons, a live iframe preview, an event tap log, and a generated CSS-style declaration block.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a kiosk builder that emits the declaration; the page does that and adds a live iframe preview.

edge_cases.rationale: Shows the iframe fallback when not in an IWA. Misses: what happens when the kiosk URL is offline, when the partition collides with another IWA, when CSS injection fails on a sandboxed iframe.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus ID 5199572022853632 elsewhere on the page. The see-also block links the feature index.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, --accent-emerald for events, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Surface partition collisions
openQuestions.detail: Two `<controlledframe>` elements with the same `partition` value share storage. That's intentional for multi-screen deployments but a gotcha for a developer expecting isolation. Add a second frame and toggle whether they share partition; show that a cookie set in one appears in the other.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate inject-on-load with `executeScript`
openQuestions.detail: The 'Inject CSS' button currently hides a nav in the iframe. The real Controlled Frame ships `executeScript({ code })`. Surface that — replace the CSS-only injection with code injection so the page teaches the bigger primitive.

openQuestions.title: Reload semantics: navigation event vs full reload
openQuestions.detail: Currently the reload button calls `iframe.contentWindow.location.reload()`. The real Controlled Frame distinguishes `frame.reload()` from `frame.src = newUrl`. Show the difference, especially how `beforeunload` interacts with each.

summary: Strong end-to-end builder that picks the right primitives (partition, nav policy, autoreload). The fallback iframe is honest but glosses over Controlled-Frame-specific behaviour (partition sharing, executeScript) that the spec actually unblocks.
